What shareholder or board resolutions are mentioned for Acer European Holdings?,"Yes. The corpus contains both board and shareholder resolutions for Acer European Holdings across several legal phases of the company’s history. The principal items I found are below.

column1: Two cautions:

1. The corpus mixes role labels across jurisdictions — “Director,” “Managing Director,” “Resident Director,” “Statutory Director,” “President,” and “Member.” I have included all board-level roles because that is how the history is recorded across entity continuations.[[cite:6123.TIF#f012ae1d80e1048333f8b830]]

2. A few older entries, especially those tied to file 3166.TIF, appear to rely on a shareholder resolution concerning Acer Europe B.V. rather than Acer European Holdings N.V. Those entries are in the board-history output, but they are weaker than the direct company-specific resolutions and registry extracts.

If useful, I can next produce this as a normalized director-only register that excludes non-director titles and flags weaker evidence separately.","Across the indexed record, the director history for the Acer European Holdings business subject spans four evidenced phases: Acer European Holdings N.V. (Netherlands Antilles/Curaçao), Acer European Holdings B.V. (Curaçao), Acer European Holdings Limited (Cyprus), and Acer European Holdings SA (Switzerland). No director evidence appears for the earlier BVI phase in the indexed materials I reviewed. column1: ## Evidence assessment
Two cautions are warranted.
1. **Some appointments are supported by direct appointment or resignation instruments; others only by registry extracts or meeting minutes.**

Direct appointment/resignation letters are the strongest support for persons such as Walter Deppeler, Tai-Yueh Lai, Oliver Ahrens, Ernest O'Leary, Gin-Ing Hu, Luca Rossi, Viveshan Ramsamy Pillay, and Emmanuel Fromont. [⁶](#corprag-source?file=3150.TIF&chunk=c844e005470792e5cea77435)[³²](#corprag-source?file=3147.TIF&chunk=b8036fc7e7ac7d569767c7d8)[²³](#corprag-source?file=5854.TIF&chunk=c7836f661ecc9b6bc31a7a2d)[²¹](#corprag-source?file=5853.TIF&chunk=3de44ef53a3a2d24da00c78e)[²⁵](#corprag-source?file=4585.TIF&chunk=6cb500965fd1c6fb1cf2eb15)[²⁸](#corprag-source?file=4629.TIF&chunk=1c87691806ae6e4e9b348ae3)[¹⁸](#corprag-source?file=4237.TIF&chunk=7ef8c38b23c061200fe2b680)[³¹](#corprag-source?file=5120.TIF&chunk=)
2. **A few names rely mainly on registry or contextual records rather than a direct appointment instrument in the indexed file.**

That applies in particular to Cheng-Tang Wang, Chun-Shen Chen, Yi-Ju Chen, Tai-Chi Shih, and parts of the Lilia Wang / Che-Min Tu transition. Those are still evidenced in the corpus, but the support is registry or meeting-record based rather than a standalone appointment letter.
